commands.author: Enominera
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Needs maintainer judgment on the stacked public history API and automatic session-family prompt carryover; there is no narrow automation repair to queue.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found; the diff adds bounded session-store reads and no new dependency, workflow, credential, or package-execution surface. (sha=85abc5a20e51656d09910f84a67eb74520b284ee)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds opt-in session-family history reads for `chat.history`/`sessions_history` and injects the latest reset-ancestor compaction summary into embedded-agent prompt context when the active context has none.

PR surface: Source +347, Tests +254, Other +4. Total +605 across 29 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence path: current `main` reads only the active chat transcript and has no embedded carryover transform, while the PR body supplies a terminal smoke for the resolver/transform. I did not run a live transport E2E because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Needs maintainer judgment on the stacked public history API and automatic session-family prompt carryover; there is no narrow automation repair to queue.

target.title: [Bug] launchd plist StandardErrorPath hardcoded to /dev/null, hides all gateway stderr (5.28 regression)
target.body:
## Summary

After 5.28, OpenClaw's generated `~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.gateway.plist` contains `<key>StandardErrorPath</key><string>/dev/null</string>`, discarding all gateway stderr (including `[prompt-cache] cache read dropped` diagnostics, model fallback warnings, liveness warnings). This is a regression from earlier versions where stderr was written to `~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.err.log` or `~/Library/Logs/openclaw/gateway.err.log`.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Install or upgrade OpenClaw (currently 2026.6.1)
2. Run `openclaw gateway install` (or rely on `rewriteLaunchAgentPlistForRestart`)
3. Inspect `~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.gateway.plist`:

   ```xml
   <key>StandardErrorPath</key>
   <string>/dev/null</string>   <!-- bug -->
   <key>StandardOutPath</key>
   <string>.../Library/Logs/openclaw/gateway.log</string>
   ```

4. Trigger any error condition in gateway (e.g., force a cache miss / fallback)
5. Run `ls -la ~/Library/Logs/openclaw/gateway.err.log` → file does not exist or stays at 0 bytes
6. Run `launchctl print ai.openclaw.gateway | grep -E "OutPath|ErrorPath"` → confirms `/dev/null`

## Expected

stderr should be written to `~/Library/Logs/openclaw/gateway.err.log` (alongside `gateway.log`), or at minimum to a real file (not `/dev/null`). Users should be able to diagnose cache / fallback / liveness issues from stderr.

## Actual

stderr is silently discarded. Diagnostics impossible for any post-5.28 incident unless the user runs gateway manually with `2> some-file` redirect.

## Root cause

`/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/launchd-DirYWoav.js` contains a hardcoded constant near the top of the file:

```js
const LAUNCH_AGENT_STDERR_PATH = "/dev/null";
```

This constant is passed as `stderrPath` to `buildLaunchAgentPlist` at two call sites in the same file (one in `writeLaunchAgentPlist`, one in `rewriteLaunchAgentPlistForRestart`), so:

- A fresh install writes `/dev/null` to plist
- A `rewriteLaunchAgentPlistForRestart` after a restart also writes `/dev/null`
- Any user-side fix to the plist gets overwritten on the next gateway restart

A `resolveMacLaunchAgentLogPaths(env)` helper already exists in `/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/restart-logs-*.js` and correctly computes `stderrPath: <logDir>/<prefix>.err.log`, but it is **not used** by the launchd plist generator.

## Impact

- All `[prompt-cache] cache read dropped` events lost → cannot diagnose cache hit rate regressions
- All `[model-fallback/decision]` and `[diagnostic]` warnings lost
- Affects every macOS user running gateway via launchd (the default install path)
- Related user reports: #56543 (Strongly +1 on `doctor --fix` detecting stale plist), #65734 (referenced the now-discontinued stderr path)

## Suggested fix

1. Remove the hardcoded `LAUNCH_AGENT_STDERR_PATH = "/dev/null"` constant
2. Pass `stderrPath: resolveMacLaunchAgentLogPaths(env).stderrPath` (or equivalent) to `buildLaunchAgentPlist`
3. Verify the resulting plist contains a real file path (not `/dev/null`)
4. (Optional) Add a `doctor --fix` check that detects plists with `StandardErrorPath = /dev/null` and repairs them

## Environment

- OpenClaw 2026.6.1 (regression introduced in 5.28)
- macOS launchd (user LaunchAgent)
- plist path: `~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.gateway.plist`

## Related

- #56543 (stale plist detection)
- #65734 (referenced previous stderr path)
- Local workaround: `plutil -replace StandardErrorPath -string "~/Library/Logs/openclaw/gateway.err.log" ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.gateway.plist` + `launchctl bootout/unload/load`
target.author: Enominera
